chunk_summary: Guidelines for feeding infants and young children under 2 years during acute diarrhea, emphasizing continued feeding, rehydration with ORS, zinc supplementation, and dehydration triage, with special considerations for malnutrition and recovery feeding.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of continuing breastfeeding and age-appropriate feeding during acute diarrhea.
Learn dehydration triage criteria and appropriate referral protocols for severe cases.
Apply zinc supplementation guidelines for infants and children with acute diarrhea.
Recognize special feeding considerations for malnourished children and post-recovery catch-up feeding.
key_facts: Continue breastfeeding and offer small, frequent portions of complementary foods during diarrhea to prevent weight loss and wasting.
Prescribe zinc supplementation for 10–14 days: 10 mg/day for infants under 6 months and 20 mg/day for older infants and children.
Urgent referral is required for severe dehydration signs such as lethargy, sunken eyes, or minimal urine output.
For formula-fed infants with persistent high-volume diarrhea, consider lactose-free formula only if clinically significant lactose intolerance is suspected.
Malnourished children require specialized rehydration protocols and monitoring due to higher risks during hydration.
reference_quote: Zinc supplementation for diarrhea is recommended for 10–14 days with age-specific dosing: 10 mg/day for infants under 6 months and 20 mg/day for older infants and children. (WHO ELENA)
